description: A high-precision rotating spindle system for machine tools, incorporating motor drive, bearing assemblies, and tool interface for machining operations.

components.name: spindle_motor
components.role: provides rotational power and precise speed control for the spindle
components.evidence: FANUC AC SPINDLE MOTOR αi series, synchronous built-in spindle motor driving

operating_ranges.evidence: Parameter 3020/4020/4020: Maximum motor speed, Valid data range: 0 to 32767
Parameter 3022/4022/4022: Speed arrival detection level, Standard setting value: 150
Parameter 3023/4023/4023: Speed detection level, Standard setting value: 30
Parameter 3024/4024/4024: Speed zero detection level, Standard setting value: 75
Parameter 3025/4025/4025: Sets a torque limitation value, Standard setting value: 50
Parameter 3026/4026/4026: Load detection level 1, Standard setting value: 83
Parameter 3027/4027/4027: Load detection level 2, Standard setting value: 95
Parameters 3040/4040/4040 and 3041/4041/4041: Velocity loop proportional gain, Standard setting: 10
Parameters 3048/4048/4048 and 3049/4049/4049: Velocity loop integral gain, Standard setting: 10
Parameter 3038/4038/4038: Orientation speed upper limit, Standard setting value: 0

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: short-time overload detection period standard: 30 seconds
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Parameter 3123/4123/4123: Short-time overload detection period, Standard setting value: 30
Parameter 3089/4089/4089: Velocity error excess detection level when the motor is rotating
SP0027: Position coder disconnected, SP0047: Position coder signal error

fault_conditions.name: motor_overheat
fault_conditions.description: Motor overheat alarm
fault_conditions.symptoms: motor temperature exceeds safe operating limits
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0001: Motor overheat

fault_conditions.name: excessive_velocity_error
fault_conditions.description: Excessive velocity error alarm
fault_conditions.symptoms: velocity error exceeds detection level
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0002: Excessive velocity error

fault_conditions.name: overspeed
fault_conditions.description: Overspeed alarm
fault_conditions.symptoms: speed exceeds maximum motor speed × 115%
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0007: Overspeed, Parameter 3087/4087/4087: Excessive speed level, Standard setting value: 115

fault_conditions.name: short_time_overload
fault_conditions.description: Short-time overload alarm
fault_conditions.symptoms: load ≥90% of maximum output for ≥30 seconds
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0029: Short-time overload

fault_conditions.name: motor_lock
fault_conditions.description: Motor lock alarm
fault_conditions.symptoms: motor binding detected
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0031: Motor lock alarm

fault_conditions.name: position_coder_disconnected
fault_conditions.description: Position coder disconnected
fault_conditions.symptoms: loss of position feedback signal
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0027: Position coder disconnected

fault_conditions.name: current_overload
fault_conditions.description: Current overload alarm
fault_conditions.symptoms: excessive current detected

maintenance_actions.name: parameter_initialization
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: all motor parameters to factory defaults
maintenance_actions.evidence: Parameter 4019#0 PRLOAD: Automatic parameter setting function performs automatic parameter setting

maintenance_actions.name: motor_power_cycle
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: motor power state and control system
maintenance_actions.evidence: Parameter 3081/4081/4081: Delay time until the motor power is turned off, Standard setting value: 20 (200ms)

assumptions.statement: Motor operates within specified speed ranges based on motor model
assumptions.reason: Different motor models have different maximum speeds (6000, 8000, 10000, 12000, 15000 min⁻¹) as shown in parameter tables

assumptions.statement: Standard parameter settings are appropriate for most applications
assumptions.reason: Manual states 'Usually, the settings of these parameters need not be changed. Use the values indicated on a parameter table for each motor model without modification'

assumptions.statement: Gear ratio is typically 1:1 unless otherwise specified
assumptions.reason: Gear ratio parameters 3056-3059/4056-4059 have standard setting of 100, representing 1:1 ratio

components.name: angular_contact_bearings
components.role: support radial and axial loads while maintaining high precision rotation
components.evidence: Angular contact ball bearings with preload settings, 15% of internal free space grease quantity

operating_ranges.evidence: Bearing Test Specifications: Bearing: 7006C Speed: 34000 min⁻¹
Preload: 4500N for Ø 30x Ø 62x Ø 15mm bearing
Temperature rise at different speeds: 1000rpm ~5°C to 3000rpm ~35°C depending on seal configuration
Suggested axial compression: 10-30μm for outer cap male register surface
Grease quantities range from 0.03 cc/brg for smallest bearings to 80 cc/brg for largest bearings

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: 90% reduced grease leakage with sealed bearings
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: 90% Reduced Grease Leakage with sealed bearings vs open bearings
Water Contamination Ratio in Grease and need to prevent water and dust ingress

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: 200 hours for brass cage vs 300 hours for PPS material under severe conditions
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: wear could be found in the Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for the PPS material
electrical resistance prevents pitting of the ball surface due to electrical discharge in built-in motor spindles

fault_conditions.name: excessive_temperature
fault_conditions.description: High temperature generation due to inadequate sealing or excessive preload
fault_conditions.symptoms: Temperature rise above normal operating range
fault_conditions.evidence: low friction design prevents high temperature generation and temperature rise comparison data

fault_conditions.name: cage_noise
fault_conditions.description: Friction between ball surface and cage guide surface in cold conditions
fault_conditions.symptoms: Audible cage noise particularly in cold conditions
fault_conditions.evidence: cage noise can occur in grease lubricated bearings due to friction between the ball surface and the cage guide surface; this can be particularly noticed in cold conditions

fault_conditions.name: grease_leakage
fault_conditions.description: Loss of lubrication due to grease escaping from bearing
fault_conditions.symptoms: 25% leakage in open bearings vs ~2% with seals
fault_conditions.evidence: Open: 25% leakage, with Seal: ~2% leakage

fault_conditions.name: contamination_ingress
fault_conditions.description: Water, dust, or coolant entering bearing causing degradation
fault_conditions.symptoms: Reduced bearing life and performance
fault_conditions.evidence: sealed bearings provide extra reliability in dusty and water/oil contaminated environments

maintenance_actions.name: grease_replenishment
maintenance_actions.evidence: The greasing process is very important to the successful performance of the bearing and grease quantities specified for different bearing sizes

maintenance_actions.name: running_in_procedure
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: bearing operating temperature and grease distribution
maintenance_actions.evidence: Do not operate bearings at full spindle speed when bearings are first installed. It is necessary to run the grease in

maintenance_actions.name: preload_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: bearing mounted preload can be reduced or increased by offsetting spacers

assumptions.statement: Sealed bearings use contacting seals with waterproof grease
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies sealed bearings have contacting type seals with special WPH waterproof grease

assumptions.statement: Hybrid bearings use ceramic balls with steel rings
assumptions.reason: Manual defines hybrid as steel ringed bearings using silicon nitride ceramic balls

assumptions.statement: Grease quantity is based on percentage of internal free space
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies 15% of internal free space for angular contact ball bearings

components.name: cylindrical_roller_bearings
components.role: handle heavy radial loads in spindle assembly
components.evidence: Double row tapered bore cylindrical roller bearing - usually slight clearance at rear, preloaded at front

operating_ranges.evidence: Front roller bearing: Zero to 3μm tight
Rear roller bearing: Slight clearance (-2 to -5μm)
NN30 X-quantity ranges from 0.4 to 69 cc/brg depending on bore size
NN39 X-quantity ranges from 4.5 to 75 cc/brg depending on bore size
NN49 X-quantity ranges from 5.4 to 44 cc/brg depending on bore size
N10 X-quantity ranges from 0.4 to 12.9 cc/brg depending on bore size
Typically 11-14% increase in speed with TB cages

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: peak life occurs at approximately zero clearance
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Graph shows life ratio from -0.008mm to +0.008mm radial clearance with peak life at zero clearance
grease discolouration due to wear on brass cage

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: wear found in Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for PPS material
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: wear could be found in the Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for the PPS material of the TB cage

fault_conditions.name: flaking_one_side_raceway
fault_conditions.description: Flaking on one side of the raceway of radial bearing
fault_conditions.symptoms: abnormal axial load (sliding failure of free-side bearing)
fault_conditions.evidence: Flaking on one side of the raceway of radial bearing caused by abnormal axial load

fault_conditions.name: scoring_raceway_rolling_surface
fault_conditions.description: Scoring or smearing between raceway and rolling surface
fault_conditions.symptoms: inadequate initial lubrication, excessively hard grease, high acceleration when starting
fault_conditions.evidence: Scoring or smearing between raceway and rolling surface due to inadequate initial lubrication

fault_conditions.name: rib_scoring
fault_conditions.description: Scoring or smearing between the end face of the rollers and guide rib
fault_conditions.symptoms: inadequate lubrication, incorrect mounting and large axial load
fault_conditions.evidence: Scoring or smearing between the end face of the rollers and guide rib

fault_conditions.name: excessive_preload
fault_conditions.description: Excessive bearing preload leading to seizure
fault_conditions.symptoms: high bearing running temperature and seizure
fault_conditions.evidence: Excessive preload (negative radial clearance) can result in high bearing running temperature and seizure

fault_conditions.name: cage_noise
fault_conditions.description: Cage noise in grease lubricated bearings
fault_conditions.symptoms: friction between ball surface and cage guide surface, particularly in cold conditions
fault_conditions.evidence: cage noise can occur in grease lubricated bearings due to friction between the ball surface and the cage guide surface

maintenance_actions.name: grease_running_in
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: grease distribution and bearing temperature stability
maintenance_actions.evidence: Do not operate bearings at full spindle speed when bearings are first installed. It is necessary to run the grease in

maintenance_actions.name: radial_clearance_adjustment
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: bearing preload and clearance to optimal values
maintenance_actions.evidence: Clearance/preload easily changed in-situ by pushing bearing up 1:12 taper

maintenance_actions.name: spacer_grinding
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: mounted radial internal clearance to target values
maintenance_actions.evidence: Grind spacer to specific width after setting radial internal clearance and before final mounting

assumptions.statement: Grease quantities are based on 10% of internal free space for cylindrical roller bearings
assumptions.reason: Standard specified in manual for cylindrical roller bearing lubrication

assumptions.statement: Target preload of 2μm for tool-side and 5μm clearance for rear-side are typical values
assumptions.reason: Manual states these as usual targets but notes they depend on OEM specs and operating conditions

assumptions.statement: TB cage speed improvement applies to NN30xx series bearings
assumptions.reason: Speed comparison data shown specifically for NN3022 bearing with TB vs MB cage

components.name: position_coder
components.role: provides precise angular position feedback for spindle orientation and control
components.evidence: α position coder connected via JYA2 and JYA3 to SPMC, 4096 pulses per revolution

operating_ranges.evidence: Position coder method orientation stop position - Unit of data : 1 pulse (360°/4096)
Valid data range : 0 to 4096
Valid data range : 0 to 32767. When this data is set to 0, 200 min⁻¹ is set.
Position gain for orientation (A parameter is selected by the CTH1A and CTH2A input signals.) Valid data range : 0 to 32767

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Position coder disconnected - SP0027
Position coder one-rotation signal detection error - SP0041
Position coder signal error - SP0047

fault_conditions.name: position_coder_disconnected
fault_conditions.description: Position coder disconnected
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0027 | 9027 | SP9027 | 27 | Position coder disconnected

fault_conditions.name: one_rotation_signal_detection_error
fault_conditions.description: Position coder one-rotation signal detection error
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0041 | 9041 | SP9041 | 41 | Position coder one-rotation signal detection error

fault_conditions.name: one_rotation_signal_not_detected
fault_conditions.description: Position coder one-rotation signal not detected
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0042 | 9042 | SP9042 | 42 | Position coder one-rotation signal not detected

fault_conditions.name: position_coder_signal_error
fault_conditions.description: Position coder signal error
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0047 | 9047 | SP9047 | 47 | Position coder signal error

maintenance_actions.name: check_position_detect_signal
maintenance_actions.evidence: D. Check the position detect signal (position coder signal).

maintenance_actions.name: adjust_orientation_stop_position
maintenance_actions.evidence: G. Adjust the orientation stop position. - Detection level for the orientation completion signal - Orientation stop position shift - Orientation stop position

maintenance_actions.name: check_position_coder_feedback
maintenance_actions.evidence: D. Connect position coder and check feedback signal

assumptions.statement: Position coder provides 4096 pulses per revolution for 360° rotation
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies unit of data as 1 pulse (360°/4096) for position coder method

assumptions.statement: Position coder must be connected directly or with 1:1 gear ratio to spindle
assumptions.reason: Manual states 'Connection to Spindle: Directly connected or connected with a gear or timing belt at a ratio of 1:1'

assumptions.statement: Position coder is the only supported separate detector for αCi series
assumptions.reason: Manual notes 'The spindle sensor (separate detector) usable with the αCi series spindle amplifier is a position coder only'

components.name: spindle_amplifier
components.role: controls motor speed, torque, and position based on CNC commands
components.evidence: SPM (Spindle Motor Control) with velocity control mode and servo mode capabilities

operating_ranges.evidence: Velocity loop proportional gain on velocity control mode (HIGH) CTH1A=0, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Velocity loop proportional gain on velocity control mode (LOW) CTH1A=1, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Velocity loop integral gain on velocity control mode (HIGH) CTH1A=0, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Velocity loop integral gain on velocity control mode (LOW) CTH1A=1, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Position gain on Cs contouring control (HIGH) CTH1A=0, CTH2A=0, Unit: 0.01sec⁻¹, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 3000
Sets a torque limitation value, Unit: 1%, Valid data range: 0 to 100, Standard setting: 50
Maximum motor speed, Unit: 1min⁻¹, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: Depends on the motor model
Acceleration used for spindle synchronous control, Unit: 1min⁻¹/sec, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 0

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: The velocity loop gain may be too large. When the motor vibrates and makes an abnormal sound when rotating, decrease the velocity loop proportional and integral gain setting values
If an abrupt application of a heavy load in the no-load condition lowers the motor speed, adjust this parameter to around 50 to 70 to improve the torque response characteristic
When the cutting capability is degraded, check parameter data for each motor model and output limitation pattern and output limitation value

fault_conditions.name: velocity_error_excess_alarm
fault_conditions.description: Velocity error exceeds alarm level during acceleration/deceleration
fault_conditions.symptoms: Motor cannot follow speed command immediately after acceleration/deceleration start
fault_conditions.evidence: velocity error exceeds the velocity error excess alarm (SPM alarm 02) level after start of acceleration/deceleration on the velocity control mode

fault_conditions.name: excessive_speed_deviation_alarm
fault_conditions.description: Speed deviation exceeds detection level in spindle synchronous control
fault_conditions.symptoms: Position error or speed difference exceeds parameter setting
fault_conditions.evidence: If positional deviation or difference between speed command and actual spindle speed exceeds value set in parameter in spindle synchronous control mode, excessive speed deviation alarm (SPM alarm C8) is detected

fault_conditions.name: excessive_positional_deviation_alarm
fault_conditions.description: Positional deviation exceeds detection level in spindle synchronous control
fault_conditions.symptoms: Position error exceeds parameter setting
fault_conditions.evidence: If positional deviation (position error) exceeds value set in parameter in spindle synchronous control mode, excessive positional deviation alarm (SPM alarm C9) is detected

fault_conditions.name: improper_gear_ratio_operation
fault_conditions.description: Unexpected operation due to improper gear ratio parameter
fault_conditions.symptoms: Spindle does not stop but keeps rotating at orientation time
fault_conditions.evidence: When an improper value is set in these parameters, an unexpected operation can occur. For example, the spindle does not stop but keeps rotating at the time of orientation

maintenance_actions.name: velocity_loop_gain_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: The velocity loop gain may be too large. Adjust the velocity loop proportional gain and integral gain parameters by decreasing the setting values

maintenance_actions.name: parameter_initialization
maintenance_actions.evidence: After setting a desired motor model code in parameter No. 4133 and setting PRLOAD bit to 1, turn off the power to the CNC, then turn on the power to the CNC again. The parameters are automatically initialized

maintenance_actions.name: motor_power_delay_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: This parameter sets a period of time from the stop of the motor until the power to the motor is turned off. When a small value is set, the motor can coast after power is turned off

assumptions.statement: Spindle amplifier controls motor through velocity and position feedback loops with adjustable gains
assumptions.reason: Multiple parameters specify velocity loop proportional/integral gains and position gains for different control modes

assumptions.statement: Control performance depends on proper parameter tuning for specific motor models
assumptions.reason: Standard settings depend on motor model and automatic parameter setting function exists for motor-specific initialization

assumptions.statement: System operates in multiple control modes with different parameter sets
assumptions.reason: Parameters are specified for velocity control mode, Cs contouring control, and spindle synchronous control with different gain settings

components.name: tool_interface
components.role: secures and positions cutting tools with precise repeatability
components.evidence: Tooling interface for tool holder ISO with drawbar, grippers, pull stud, taper angle

operating_ranges.evidence: Parameter 3075/4075/4075 - Detection level for the spindle orientation completion signal, Valid data range: 0 to 100, Standard setting value: 10
Parameters 3060-3063/4060-4063 - Position gain for orientation, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 1000
Parameter 3038/4038/4038 - Spindle orientation speed, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 0
Parameter 3077/4077/4077 - Orientation stop position shift value, Valid data range: -4095 to 4095, Standard setting: 0
Parameters 3042/4042 and 3043/4043 - Velocity loop proportional gain on orientation, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: When an overshoot or hunting occurs - The velocity loop gain may be too large. Adjust the following parameters: Velocity proportional gain, Velocity integral gain
When a small value is set in this parameter, the motor can coast after the power to the motor is turned off
When an improper value is set in these parameters, an unexpected operation can occur. For example, the spindle can continue rotating without stopping at the time of orientation

assumptions.statement: Tool interface operates in position coder method orientation mode
assumptions.reason: Manual sections focus extensively on position coder method spindle orientation with detailed parameter tables

assumptions.statement: Precise repeatability depends on proper gear ratio configuration
assumptions.reason: Multiple warnings about improper gear ratio settings causing unexpected operations and continued rotation without stopping

assumptions.statement: System uses FANUC αi or Bis series spindle motors
assumptions.reason: All parameter references and technical specifications are specific to these motor series

components.name: housing_assembly
components.role: provides structural support and precise mounting for all spindle components
components.evidence: Spindle housing with bearing mounting surfaces, lubrication channels, cooling systems

operating_ranges.evidence: Most spindle designs include an outer cap whose male register surface should lightly compress the bearing's outer ring. Suggested axial compression: 10-30μm.
Target outer ring to housing fit: 2μm loose to 2μm tight
General Target: 3 to 5 microns (depends upon application)

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Excessive and/or uneven clamping pressure can result in bearing noise or loss of preload
Protection against contamination from cutting operations (cutting fluid and chips)
Rear-side bearings and shaft must float within housing bore to allow axial thermal expansion

fault_conditions.name: excessive_bearing_preload
fault_conditions.description: Excessive bearing-shaft interference or insufficient bearing-housing clearance fit may lead to excessive bearing preload and seizure
fault_conditions.symptoms: bearing noise or loss of preload
fault_conditions.evidence: Excessive bearing-shaft interference or insufficient bearing-housing clearance fit may lead to excessive bearing preload and seizure

fault_conditions.name: thermal_expansion_restriction
fault_conditions.description: Insufficient bearing-housing clearance at the rear-side may prevent the rear-side bearings and shaft from floating within housing bore during axial thermal expansion
fault_conditions.evidence: Insufficient bearing-housing clearance at the rear-side may prevent the rear-side bearings and shaft from floating within housing bore during axial thermal expansion

fault_conditions.name: contamination_ingress
fault_conditions.description: Entry of foreign matter such as metallic particles and grit
fault_conditions.symptoms: Indentations on raceway and rolling elements
fault_conditions.evidence: Entry of foreign matter such as metallic particles and grit

maintenance_actions.name: housing_runout_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: Housing Measurements Target Values: General Target: 3 to 5 microns (depends upon application)

maintenance_actions.name: housing_bore_measurement
maintenance_actions.evidence: Measure the shaft OD and housing bore at the bearing locations. Calculate the bearing shaft and housing fits and compare with OEM specifications or NSK guidelines

maintenance_actions.name: sealing_system_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: Labyrinth-type sealing with narrow grooves tightly adjusted to housing walls. Air sealing systems with constant controlled pressured air feed

assumptions.statement: Housing provides stable thermal environment for bearing operation
assumptions.reason: Manual discusses thermal expansion accommodation but does not specify housing thermal properties

assumptions.statement: Housing geometry tolerances follow ISO 1101 standard
assumptions.reason: Manual references ISO 1101 standard for geometry and dimensional tolerances including runout, angularity, circularity, concentricity

assumptions.statement: Motor operates within specified speed ranges based on motor model

assumptions.statement: Standard parameter settings are appropriate for most applications
assumptions.reason: Manual states 'Usually, the settings of these parameters need not be changed. Use the values indicated on a parameter table for each motor model without modification'

assumptions.statement: Gear ratio is typically 1:1 unless otherwise specified
assumptions.reason: Gear ratio parameters 3056-3059/4056-4059 have standard setting of 100, representing 1:1 ratio

operating_ranges.evidence: Bearing Test Specifications: Bearing: 7006C Speed: 34000 min⁻¹
Preload: 4500N for Ø 30x Ø 62x Ø 15mm bearing
Temperature rise at different speeds: 1000rpm ~5°C to 3000rpm ~35°C depending on seal configuration
Suggested axial compression: 10-30μm for outer cap male register surface
Grease quantities range from 0.03 cc/brg for smallest bearings to 80 cc/brg for largest bearings

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: 90% reduced grease leakage with sealed bearings
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: 90% Reduced Grease Leakage with sealed bearings vs open bearings
Water Contamination Ratio in Grease and need to prevent water and dust ingress

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: 200 hours for brass cage vs 300 hours for PPS material under severe conditions
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: wear could be found in the Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for the PPS material
electrical resistance prevents pitting of the ball surface due to electrical discharge in built-in motor spindles

fault_conditions.name: excessive_temperature
fault_conditions.description: High temperature generation due to inadequate sealing or excessive preload
fault_conditions.symptoms: Temperature rise above normal operating range
fault_conditions.evidence: low friction design prevents high temperature generation and temperature rise comparison data

fault_conditions.name: cage_noise
fault_conditions.description: Friction between ball surface and cage guide surface in cold conditions
fault_conditions.symptoms: Audible cage noise particularly in cold conditions
fault_conditions.evidence: cage noise can occur in grease lubricated bearings due to friction between the ball surface and the cage guide surface; this can be particularly noticed in cold conditions

fault_conditions.name: grease_leakage
fault_conditions.description: Loss of lubrication due to grease escaping from bearing
fault_conditions.symptoms: 25% leakage in open bearings vs ~2% with seals
fault_conditions.evidence: Open: 25% leakage, with Seal: ~2% leakage

fault_conditions.name: contamination_ingress
fault_conditions.description: Water, dust, or coolant entering bearing causing degradation
fault_conditions.symptoms: Reduced bearing life and performance
fault_conditions.evidence: sealed bearings provide extra reliability in dusty and water/oil contaminated environments

maintenance_actions.name: grease_replenishment
maintenance_actions.evidence: The greasing process is very important to the successful performance of the bearing and grease quantities specified for different bearing sizes

maintenance_actions.name: running_in_procedure
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: bearing operating temperature and grease distribution
maintenance_actions.evidence: Do not operate bearings at full spindle speed when bearings are first installed. It is necessary to run the grease in

maintenance_actions.name: preload_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: bearing mounted preload can be reduced or increased by offsetting spacers

assumptions.statement: Sealed bearings use contacting seals with waterproof grease
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies sealed bearings have contacting type seals with special WPH waterproof grease

assumptions.statement: Hybrid bearings use ceramic balls with steel rings
assumptions.reason: Manual defines hybrid as steel ringed bearings using silicon nitride ceramic balls

assumptions.statement: Grease quantity is based on percentage of internal free space
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies 15% of internal free space for angular contact ball bearings

operating_ranges.evidence: Front roller bearing: Zero to 3μm tight
Rear roller bearing: Slight clearance (-2 to -5μm)
NN30 X-quantity ranges from 0.4 to 69 cc/brg depending on bore size
NN39 X-quantity ranges from 4.5 to 75 cc/brg depending on bore size
NN49 X-quantity ranges from 5.4 to 44 cc/brg depending on bore size
N10 X-quantity ranges from 0.4 to 12.9 cc/brg depending on bore size
Typically 11-14% increase in speed with TB cages

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: peak life occurs at approximately zero clearance
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Graph shows life ratio from -0.008mm to +0.008mm radial clearance with peak life at zero clearance
grease discolouration due to wear on brass cage

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: wear found in Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for PPS material
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: wear could be found in the Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for the PPS material of the TB cage

fault_conditions.name: flaking_one_side_raceway
fault_conditions.description: Flaking on one side of the raceway of radial bearing
fault_conditions.symptoms: abnormal axial load (sliding failure of free-side bearing)
fault_conditions.evidence: Flaking on one side of the raceway of radial bearing caused by abnormal axial load

fault_conditions.name: scoring_raceway_rolling_surface
fault_conditions.description: Scoring or smearing between raceway and rolling surface
fault_conditions.symptoms: inadequate initial lubrication, excessively hard grease, high acceleration when starting
fault_conditions.evidence: Scoring or smearing between raceway and rolling surface due to inadequate initial lubrication

fault_conditions.name: rib_scoring
fault_conditions.description: Scoring or smearing between the end face of the rollers and guide rib
fault_conditions.symptoms: inadequate lubrication, incorrect mounting and large axial load
fault_conditions.evidence: Scoring or smearing between the end face of the rollers and guide rib

fault_conditions.name: excessive_preload
fault_conditions.description: Excessive bearing preload leading to seizure
fault_conditions.symptoms: high bearing running temperature and seizure
fault_conditions.evidence: Excessive preload (negative radial clearance) can result in high bearing running temperature and seizure

fault_conditions.name: cage_noise
fault_conditions.description: Cage noise in grease lubricated bearings
fault_conditions.symptoms: friction between ball surface and cage guide surface, particularly in cold conditions
fault_conditions.evidence: cage noise can occur in grease lubricated bearings due to friction between the ball surface and the cage guide surface

maintenance_actions.name: grease_running_in
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: grease distribution and bearing temperature stability
maintenance_actions.evidence: Do not operate bearings at full spindle speed when bearings are first installed. It is necessary to run the grease in

maintenance_actions.name: radial_clearance_adjustment
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: bearing preload and clearance to optimal values
maintenance_actions.evidence: Clearance/preload easily changed in-situ by pushing bearing up 1:12 taper

maintenance_actions.name: spacer_grinding
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: mounted radial internal clearance to target values
maintenance_actions.evidence: Grind spacer to specific width after setting radial internal clearance and before final mounting

assumptions.statement: Grease quantities are based on 10% of internal free space for cylindrical roller bearings
assumptions.reason: Standard specified in manual for cylindrical roller bearing lubrication

assumptions.statement: Target preload of 2μm for tool-side and 5μm clearance for rear-side are typical values
assumptions.reason: Manual states these as usual targets but notes they depend on OEM specs and operating conditions

assumptions.statement: TB cage speed improvement applies to NN30xx series bearings
assumptions.reason: Speed comparison data shown specifically for NN3022 bearing with TB vs MB cage

maintenance_actions.name: check_position_detect_signal
maintenance_actions.evidence: D. Check the position detect signal (position coder signal).

maintenance_actions.name: adjust_orientation_stop_position
maintenance_actions.evidence: G. Adjust the orientation stop position. - Detection level for the orientation completion signal - Orientation stop position shift - Orientation stop position

maintenance_actions.evidence: D. Connect position coder and check feedback signal

assumptions.statement: Position coder provides 4096 pulses per revolution for 360° rotation
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies unit of data as 1 pulse (360°/4096) for position coder method

assumptions.statement: Position coder must be connected directly or with 1:1 gear ratio to spindle
assumptions.reason: Manual states 'Connection to Spindle: Directly connected or connected with a gear or timing belt at a ratio of 1:1'

assumptions.statement: Position coder is the only supported separate detector for αCi series
assumptions.reason: Manual notes 'The spindle sensor (separate detector) usable with the αCi series spindle amplifier is a position coder only'

operating_ranges.evidence: Velocity loop proportional gain on velocity control mode (HIGH) CTH1A=0, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Velocity loop proportional gain on velocity control mode (LOW) CTH1A=1, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Velocity loop integral gain on velocity control mode (HIGH) CTH1A=0, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Velocity loop integral gain on velocity control mode (LOW) CTH1A=1, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10
Position gain on Cs contouring control (HIGH) CTH1A=0, CTH2A=0, Unit: 0.01sec⁻¹, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 3000
Sets a torque limitation value, Unit: 1%, Valid data range: 0 to 100, Standard setting: 50
Maximum motor speed, Unit: 1min⁻¹, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: Depends on the motor model
Acceleration used for spindle synchronous control, Unit: 1min⁻¹/sec, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 0

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: The velocity loop gain may be too large. When the motor vibrates and makes an abnormal sound when rotating, decrease the velocity loop proportional and integral gain setting values
If an abrupt application of a heavy load in the no-load condition lowers the motor speed, adjust this parameter to around 50 to 70 to improve the torque response characteristic
When the cutting capability is degraded, check parameter data for each motor model and output limitation pattern and output limitation value

fault_conditions.name: velocity_error_excess_alarm
fault_conditions.description: Velocity error exceeds alarm level during acceleration/deceleration
fault_conditions.symptoms: Motor cannot follow speed command immediately after acceleration/deceleration start
fault_conditions.evidence: velocity error exceeds the velocity error excess alarm (SPM alarm 02) level after start of acceleration/deceleration on the velocity control mode

fault_conditions.name: excessive_speed_deviation_alarm
fault_conditions.description: Speed deviation exceeds detection level in spindle synchronous control
fault_conditions.symptoms: Position error or speed difference exceeds parameter setting
fault_conditions.evidence: If positional deviation or difference between speed command and actual spindle speed exceeds value set in parameter in spindle synchronous control mode, excessive speed deviation alarm (SPM alarm C8) is detected

fault_conditions.name: excessive_positional_deviation_alarm
fault_conditions.description: Positional deviation exceeds detection level in spindle synchronous control
fault_conditions.symptoms: Position error exceeds parameter setting
fault_conditions.evidence: If positional deviation (position error) exceeds value set in parameter in spindle synchronous control mode, excessive positional deviation alarm (SPM alarm C9) is detected

fault_conditions.name: improper_gear_ratio_operation
fault_conditions.description: Unexpected operation due to improper gear ratio parameter
fault_conditions.symptoms: Spindle does not stop but keeps rotating at orientation time
fault_conditions.evidence: When an improper value is set in these parameters, an unexpected operation can occur. For example, the spindle does not stop but keeps rotating at the time of orientation

maintenance_actions.name: velocity_loop_gain_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: The velocity loop gain may be too large. Adjust the velocity loop proportional gain and integral gain parameters by decreasing the setting values

maintenance_actions.name: parameter_initialization
maintenance_actions.evidence: After setting a desired motor model code in parameter No. 4133 and setting PRLOAD bit to 1, turn off the power to the CNC, then turn on the power to the CNC again. The parameters are automatically initialized

maintenance_actions.name: motor_power_delay_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: This parameter sets a period of time from the stop of the motor until the power to the motor is turned off. When a small value is set, the motor can coast after power is turned off

assumptions.statement: Spindle amplifier controls motor through velocity and position feedback loops with adjustable gains
assumptions.reason: Multiple parameters specify velocity loop proportional/integral gains and position gains for different control modes

assumptions.statement: Control performance depends on proper parameter tuning for specific motor models
assumptions.reason: Standard settings depend on motor model and automatic parameter setting function exists for motor-specific initialization

assumptions.statement: System operates in multiple control modes with different parameter sets
assumptions.reason: Parameters are specified for velocity control mode, Cs contouring control, and spindle synchronous control with different gain settings

operating_ranges.evidence: Parameter 3075/4075/4075 - Detection level for the spindle orientation completion signal, Valid data range: 0 to 100, Standard setting value: 10
Parameters 3060-3063/4060-4063 - Position gain for orientation, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 1000
Parameter 3038/4038/4038 - Spindle orientation speed, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 0
Parameter 3077/4077/4077 - Orientation stop position shift value, Valid data range: -4095 to 4095, Standard setting: 0
Parameters 3042/4042 and 3043/4043 - Velocity loop proportional gain on orientation, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: When an overshoot or hunting occurs - The velocity loop gain may be too large. Adjust the following parameters: Velocity proportional gain, Velocity integral gain
When a small value is set in this parameter, the motor can coast after the power to the motor is turned off
When an improper value is set in these parameters, an unexpected operation can occur. For example, the spindle can continue rotating without stopping at the time of orientation

assumptions.statement: Tool interface operates in position coder method orientation mode
assumptions.reason: Manual sections focus extensively on position coder method spindle orientation with detailed parameter tables

assumptions.statement: Precise repeatability depends on proper gear ratio configuration
assumptions.reason: Multiple warnings about improper gear ratio settings causing unexpected operations and continued rotation without stopping

assumptions.statement: System uses FANUC αi or Bis series spindle motors
assumptions.reason: All parameter references and technical specifications are specific to these motor series

operating_ranges.evidence: Most spindle designs include an outer cap whose male register surface should lightly compress the bearing's outer ring. Suggested axial compression: 10-30μm.
Target outer ring to housing fit: 2μm loose to 2μm tight
General Target: 3 to 5 microns (depends upon application)

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Excessive and/or uneven clamping pressure can result in bearing noise or loss of preload
Protection against contamination from cutting operations (cutting fluid and chips)
Rear-side bearings and shaft must float within housing bore to allow axial thermal expansion

fault_conditions.name: excessive_bearing_preload
fault_conditions.description: Excessive bearing-shaft interference or insufficient bearing-housing clearance fit may lead to excessive bearing preload and seizure
fault_conditions.symptoms: bearing noise or loss of preload
fault_conditions.evidence: Excessive bearing-shaft interference or insufficient bearing-housing clearance fit may lead to excessive bearing preload and seizure

fault_conditions.name: thermal_expansion_restriction
fault_conditions.description: Insufficient bearing-housing clearance at the rear-side may prevent the rear-side bearings and shaft from floating within housing bore during axial thermal expansion
fault_conditions.evidence: Insufficient bearing-housing clearance at the rear-side may prevent the rear-side bearings and shaft from floating within housing bore during axial thermal expansion

fault_conditions.name: contamination_ingress
fault_conditions.description: Entry of foreign matter such as metallic particles and grit
fault_conditions.symptoms: Indentations on raceway and rolling elements
fault_conditions.evidence: Entry of foreign matter such as metallic particles and grit

maintenance_actions.name: housing_runout_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: Housing Measurements Target Values: General Target: 3 to 5 microns (depends upon application)

maintenance_actions.name: housing_bore_measurement
maintenance_actions.evidence: Measure the shaft OD and housing bore at the bearing locations. Calculate the bearing shaft and housing fits and compare with OEM specifications or NSK guidelines

maintenance_actions.name: sealing_system_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: Labyrinth-type sealing with narrow grooves tightly adjusted to housing walls. Air sealing systems with constant controlled pressured air feed

assumptions.statement: Housing provides stable thermal environment for bearing operation
assumptions.reason: Manual discusses thermal expansion accommodation but does not specify housing thermal properties

assumptions.statement: Housing geometry tolerances follow ISO 1101 standard
assumptions.reason: Manual references ISO 1101 standard for geometry and dimensional tolerances including runout, angularity, circularity, concentricity
